item.title: A new video from the White House mixes Call of Duty footage with actual video of Iran strikes
item.description:
Source: The Verge
On Wednesday, the White House posted a video of actual military strikes on Iran in the style usually seen in Call of Duty highlight videos, and started the video with a clip from Call of Duty. The real-life footage of missiles and other munitions hitting targets in Iran shows clips seen in other Trump administration […]
Matched: court(+2), war(+3)

item.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it answers
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Tim Sweeney signed away his right to criticize Google until 2032
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Epic CEO Tim Sweeney might be one of the most outspoken people in the history of the world. He fought two of the world's most valuable and powerful companies almost all the way to the US Supreme Court, insulting them again and again: "crooked," "deceitful," "insanely sneaky," calling Android a "fake open platform," calling both […]
Matched: court(+2), ai(+3)

item.title: Here’s how Google describes its fee-reducing Apps Experience and Games Level Up programs
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Today, Google killed its 30 percent app store fee, partially uncoupled Google Play from Google Play Billing after they were declared an illegal monopoly in the US, and much more. From July, depending on where you live, Google will now generally charge developers 20 percent for in-app purchases, or 10 percent for subscriptions - but […]
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this week
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
From a new iPhone to a budget-friendly MacBook, Apple announced a series of new products this week.
Matched: budget(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Father sues Google, claiming Gemini chatbot drove son into fatal delusion
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A father is suing Google and Alphabet, alleging its Gemini chatbot reinforced his son’s delusional belief it was his AI wife and coached him toward suicide and a planned airport attack.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Console exclusives might be making a comeback
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The near future of game consoles could look a lot like the past. Once a hallmark of the industry, over the last few years console-exclusive games have steadily become rare, as the likes of Sony and Microsoft experimented with offering titles on multiple platforms. Heck, who knows what an Xbox even is anymore? But it […]
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Decagon completes first tender offer at $4.5B valuation
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The AI-powered customer support startup is the latest example of a fast-growing, young company that's providing employee liquidity.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: One startup’s pitch to provide more reliable AI answers: Crowdsource the chatbots
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
CollectivIQ looks to give users more accurate answers to their AI queries by showing them responses that pull information from ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok — and up to 10 other models — all at the same time.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: Who needs data centers in space when they can float offshore?
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Offshore wind developer Aikido will deploy a small data center beneath a floating offshore wind turbine later this year.
Matched: ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Eight Sleep raises $50M at $1.5B valuation
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Eight Sleep said it was free-cash-flow positive in 2025 and plans to use the new funding for new products, global expansions, and clinical validation.
Matched: ai(+3), funding(+2)

item.title: Did Live Nation punish a venue by taking Billie Eilish away?
item.description:
Source: The Verge
John Abbamondi had orders to let the CEO of Ticketmaster down easy. In April 2021, Abbamondi was the CEO of BSE Global, the company that ran Brooklyn arena the Barclays Center. BSE Global's existing Ticketmaster contract would expire at the end of September, and Abbamondi and his team had evaluated proposals from SeatGeek, AXS, and […]
Matched: policy(+3)

item.title: Seven tech giants signed Trump’s pledge to keep electricity costs from spiking around data centers
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Leaders from Google, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, OpenAI, Amazon, and xAI met with President Donald Trump today to sign a "rate payer protection pledge." It's one way they're responding to growing bipartisan concerns about electricity rates rising as tech companies and the Trump administration rush to build out a new generation of AI data centers. "[Tech […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies,’ report says
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Anthropic gave up its contract with the Pentagon over AI safety disagreements -- then, OpenAI swooped in.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Epic and Google have signed a special deal for a new class of ‘metaverse’ apps
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Epic Games and Google are burying the hatchet, but documents released today reveal that they aren't only aligned on how Google is shaking things up for app stores. The two companies have also agreed to terms about a new class of apps that they're calling "metaverse browsers," according to a heavily redacted section of a […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Apple Music to add Transparency Tags to distinguish AI music, says report
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The label or distributor has to opt in to tagging their music as AI, so it's unclear how effective this intervention will be.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: NotebookLM can now summarize research in ‘cinematic’ video overviews
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google's NotebookLM can now turn users' research and notes into fully animated "cinematic" videos, going a step further than the original video overview feature Google introduced last year. Previously, video overviews could only generate narrated slideshows, but the upgraded video overview feature uses a combination of Google's AI models, "including Gemini 3, Nano Banana Pro […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: The new MacBook Air debuts with a $50 gift card as the M4 model hits its best price
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Powered by the new M5 chip, Apple’s latest MacBook Airs are more powerful than ever with double the base storage (512GB), but they also cost $100 more than their predecessor. Fortunately, though, we’ve found a few ways to save. Best Buy is offering the new 13-inch M5-powered MacBook Air for $1,099 with a $50 gift […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: The world’s biggest automaker has one of the dirtiest supply chains: report
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Tesla, Ford, and Volvo occupy the top three spots in a new ranking of 18 global automakers based on their efforts to eliminate carbon emissions, environmental harms, and human rights violations from their supply chains. Toyota, meanwhile, lurks near the bottom of the list, underscoring the persistent difficulty in getting the world's largest car company […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: His house burned down. He used the insurance money to build PopSockets.
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Does a consumer hardware company need to get on the VC treadmill to succeed? Eleven years and 290 million products sold across 115 countries later, PopSockets has proven that the bootstrapped, low-dilution path more viable than the industry gives it credit for. The global consumer hardware brand was built on less than $500k, no institutional capital, and a philosophy professor’s determination. Watch as founder and former CEO […]
Matched: war(+3)

item.title: US and EU police shut down LeakBase, a site accused of sharing stolen passwords and hacking tools
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Authorities say LeakBase was "one of the world’s largest online forums for cybercriminals," and maintained an archive of hacked databases containing hundreds of millions of passwords.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Canvas in AI Mode is available to U.S. users in English for creating plans, projects, apps, and more.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Host a Side Event during TechCrunch Founder Summit Week in Boston
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Want to tap into the energy of 1,100+ startup founders, investors, and tech leaders descending on Boston for the Founder Summit 2026 on June 9? Host your own Side Event during “Founder Summit Week,” happening June 4-10!
Matched: startup(+2)
